DEFENDANT MANIEGO MEDIA LLC DBA CREATIVE WEST'S Motion To Enforce Deposition Subpoena For Plaintiffs Mental Health Records

Defendant's motion to enforce deposition subpoena is granted. Kaiser Permanente is ordered to produce plaintiff's mental health records. The request for an order to compel plaintiff to execute a HIPAA waiver is provisionally denied, subject to citation of authority supporting the Court's authority to so order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem James Fleming at jameshfleming@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Defendants Form And Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Dec-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Josh Dowell, Esq. (805.272.4001) of McElroy Parris Trial Lawyers for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Counsel for the non-moving party, Plaintiff, appeared at the hearing and purported not to stipulate to the pro tem hearing the matter, but was informed by the clerk that, by virtue of not contesting the tentative, Plaintiff had stipulated to the pro tem and to the tentative becoming final.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MANIEGO MEDIA LLC DBA CREATIVE WEST's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Defendants Form And Special Interrogatories:

As to all form interrogatories except for form interrogatory 6.7(c), the Motion is DENIED AS MOOT.

As to form interrogatory 6.7(c), the Motion is GRANTED. Within 15 days of notice of entry of this order, Plaintiff must either amend his response to state the nature and duration of the treatment identified in his current response to this form interrogatory or produce the documents his current response to this form interrogatory states have been produced.

As to special interrogatories 8 through 11, the Motion is GRANTED. Within 15 days of notice of entry of this order, Plaintiffs must provide the information sought by these special interrogatories that is reasonably available to him.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
